name: Sausage Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
2. Add the shortening to the dry ingredients. Cut with a pastry cutter or fingers until it resembles coarse meal.
3. Gradually add milk while stirring until the dough comes together.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead 3 or 4 times.
5. Roll the dough into an 18x12 inch rectangle on a lightly floured surface.
6. Spread hot sausage (at room temperature) over the dough, leaving about half an inch of margin on all sides.
7. Using rolling pins, carefully roll the dough lengthwise, like a jellyroll, enclosing the sausage completely.
8. Pinch and seal the seams and ends securely.
9. Cover the pinwheels with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour to allow the flavors to meld and the dough to firm up.
10. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
11. Slice the cooled pinwheels into 1/4 inch thick pieces using a sharp knife.
12. Place the slices on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them evenly.
13. Bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
Randy Rigg
